nodes.label: AI as time-saving tool
nodes.grounding_span: I only really ask AI if I'm in a time crunch and need a more efficient way of doing something

nodes.label: AI as conceptual clarifier for research
nodes.grounding_span: ask ai what it means and to explain it in a way for a college chem major to understand

nodes.label: AI as systematic troubleshooter
nodes.grounding_span: ask for the ai to do troubleshooting if my reaction doesn't go as planned

nodes.label: AI for summarization and research
nodes.label_negative: Manual reading and comprehension of full papers
nodes.grounding_span: used AI to summarize the findings, intro, results/data, and other parts of the papers

nodes.label: AI as efficient quiz/test creator
nodes.label_negative: Manual creation of exam questions and activities
nodes.grounding_span: coming up with questions to use on exam prep materials, ideas for in class activities like demos

nodes.label: AI use in core academic work (writing, analysis) as ethical concern
nodes.grounding_span: my research groups approve the use of ethical AI

nodes.label: AI use in education
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think this is good for all scenarios

nodes.label: AI as clinical information source
nodes.label_negative: AI replacing human doctor emotional benefit
nodes.grounding_span: AI has the capacity to help doctors diagnose conditions and make up treatment plans pretty efficiently

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: more efficient than reading the papers myself

nodes.label: Learning and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: understand/clarify concepts in the research group

nodes.label: Ethical and responsible AI use
nodes.grounding_span: approve the use of ethical AI

nodes.label: Human touch in healthcare
nodes.grounding_span: emotional benefit of having a human doctor vs a robot one

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI summaries
nodes.grounding_span: The AI summarized it pretty well and although I had to manually copy and paste the text into the bot, it was still a bit more efficient

nodes.label: Frustration with student AI misuse
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think this is good for all scenarios

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as partner in patient assessment
nodes.grounding_span: AI can be a pretty useful study tool in med school in its most basic forms

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI diagnostic help
nodes.grounding_span: AI has the capacity to help doctors diagnose conditions and make up treatment plans pretty efficiently

nodes.label: Context-dependent selective AI use — uses AI only when time-pressed or for specific clarifications
nodes.grounding_span: I only really ask AI if I'm in a time crunch and need a more efficient way of doing something

nodes.label: Preference for single-turn interactions unless task complexity requires follow-up
nodes.grounding_span: Unless the task is somewhat complicated ... I usually just ask the ai once and take the results to deal with myself
